back_translation: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
'Consecrate to me every firstborn, the one opening every womb among the sons of Israel — among man and beast — it belongs to me.'

Moses said to the people, 'Remember this day, on which you came out of Egypt, from the house of slavery; for by strength of hand the LORD brought you out from here, and nothing leavened shall be eaten.'
'Today you are going out, in the month of Abib.'

'When the LORD brings you to the land of the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Amorite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite, which he swore to your fathers to give you — a land flowing with milk and honey — you shall perform this service in this month.'
'Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day a feast to the LORD.'
'Unleavened bread shall be eaten the seven days; no leavened thing shall be seen with you, nor leaven seen with you in all your territory.'
'You shall tell your son on that day, saying: It is because of what the LORD did for me when I came out of Egypt.'
'It shall be for you a sign on your hand and a remembrance between your eyes, so that the law of the LORD may be in your mouth; for with a mighty hand the LORD brought you out from Egypt.'
'You shall keep this statute at its appointed time from year to year.'
'When the LORD brings you to the land of the Canaanite, as he swore to you and to your fathers, and gives it to you,'
'you shall set apart to the LORD every one opening the womb; every firstborn of the offspring of cattle that you have, the males, are for the LORD.'
'Every firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb; if you do not redeem it, then you shall break its neck. Every firstborn of man among your sons you shall redeem.'
'When your son asks you in time to come, saying, What is this? you shall say to him: With a mighty hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt, from the house of slavery.'

'When Pharaoh made himself hard to send us out, the LORD killed every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of man to the firstborn of beast. Therefore I sacrifice to the LORD every male opener of the womb, and every firstborn of my sons I redeem.'
'It shall be for a sign on your hand and for frontlets between your eyes, for with a mighty hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt.'
When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them by the way of the land of the Philistines, although it was near; for God said, 'Lest the people repent when they see war and return to Egypt.'
So God led the people around by the way of the wilderness toward the Sea of Reeds. The sons of Israel went up arrayed for battle from the land of Egypt.
Moses took the bones of Joseph with him, for he had solemnly sworn the sons of Israel, saying, 'God will surely visit you; you shall carry my bones up from here with you.'
They journeyed from Succoth and camped at Etham at the edge of the wilderness.
The LORD went before them by day in a pillar of cloud to guide them on the way, and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light, that they might travel by day and night.
The pillar of cloud by day and the pillar of fire by night did not depart from before the people.
